summary: Round 2 resolves both round-1 majors and the two fixed minors. `multipleImagesAndAudio_preservesInputOrderWithinGroup` exercises 2+2 multi-item lists with distinguishable audio payloads; partial-mix matrix (text+images, text+audio, images+audio) is covered; PNG assertion now checks the 4-byte magic signature via `isPng()`; whitespace-trim behavior is pinned by a comment in both the test and the builder, with `isNotBlank()` replacing `text.trim().isNotEmpty()`. The audio ByteArray reference-sharing minor was explicitly deferred as out of AC scope — acceptable. One narrow residual: the new multi-item test uses two identical `makeBitmap()` calls, so image order is not independently pinned (identical 2x2 ARGB bitmaps produce identical PNG bytes); an images-reversed regression would slip. Minor only — not a promotion trigger.

findings.issue: `multipleImagesAndAudio_preservesInputOrderWithinGroup` pins audio order with distinguishable byte arrays (`byteArrayOf(10,11,12)` vs `byteArrayOf(20,21,22)`) but uses two identical `makeBitmap()` (both 2x2 ARGB_8888) for images. Identical source bitmaps produce byte-identical PNGs, so a regression that reversed images, dropped `img2` and duplicated `img1`, or built from `images.asReversed()` would pass every assertion in this test. Audio is covered; images are not. Round-1 recommendation explicitly suggested distinguishable bitmaps (different dimensions) for this reason.
findings.recommendation: Make the two bitmaps distinguishable so per-slot byte comparison catches reordering. Option A: `val img1 = Bitmap.createBitmap(2, 2, Bitmap.Config.ARGB_8888); val img2 = Bitmap.createBitmap(3, 3, Bitmap.Config.ARGB_8888)` — differing dimensions yield different PNG byte lengths. Then add `val b0 = (result[0] as Content.ImageBytes).bytes; val b1 = (result[1] as Content.ImageBytes).bytes; assertTrue(b0.size != b1.size)` plus `assertArrayEquals(img1.toPngBytes(), b0)` (duplicate the private helper in the test) or simply assert `b0.size < b1.size` to pin order. Option B: mutate one bitmap (e.g. `img2.setPixel(0, 0, Color.RED)`) before building and compare sizes. Either locks image-list order the same way audio order is locked today.
